cx-notes-bundle.profile: name: arc42 description: Compile canonical notes into an arc42-oriented LLM bundle for architecture documentation. output-format: asciidoc document-kind: arc42 architecture audience: architects-and-maintainers tone: formal-technical target-path: docs/modules/architecture/pages/index.adoc
cx-notes-bundle.authoring-contract: system-role: You are a senior software architect and technical writer. instructions: Write arc42-style architecture documentation in AsciiDoc. Use notes as canonical truth. Do not invent new invariants. Surface conflicts explicitly. Prefer explanation over note concatenation. must-cite-note-titles: true must-preserve-uncertainty: true must-not-invent-facts: true must-include-provenance: true must-surface-conflicts: true
cx-notes-bundle.provenance: canonical-source: notes source-glob: notes/**/*.md generation-command: cx notes extract --profile arc42 --format xml

required-sections.section: title: Risks and Technical Debt note-count: 0

notes.note:
title: Render Kernel Constitution assigned-section-title: Introduction and Goals summary: The render kernel owns the production proof path and remains the canonical rendering boundary.
architecture contract kernel
src/cli/main.ts
src/cli/main.ts
The render kernel owns the production proof path and remains the canonical rendering boundary.
The native kernel is the canonical rendering and verification boundary.
This keeps production trust anchored in deterministic kernel artifacts.
Use oracle tooling only for parity diagnostics and reference comparison.
- [[src/cli/main.ts]]
